{{Short description|Electromagnetic radiation visible to the human eye}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Light
| image = Prism-rainbow.svg
| caption = White light [[dispersion (optics)|dispersed]] by a [[prism (optics)|prism]] into the [[visible spectrum]].
| topics = [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Optics]], [[Photon]], [[Speed of light]]
| speed = 299,792,458 m/s (in vacuum)
| wavelength_range = ~380–750 nm (visible)
}}

'''Light''' or '''visible light''' is [[electromagnetic radiation]] within the portion of the [[electromagnetic spectrum]] that is perceived by the [[human eye]]. Light exhibits both wave-like and particle-like properties ([[wave–particle duality]]). As a wave, light is characterized by its [[wavelength]] <math>\lambda</math>, [[frequency]] <math>\nu</math>, and [[amplitude]]. As a particle, light consists of discrete quanta called [[photon]]s.

The primary properties of light are [[intensity (physics)|intensity]], propagation direction, [[frequency]] or wavelength [[electromagnetic spectrum|spectrum]], and [[polarization (waves)|polarization]]. Its [[speed of light|speed]] in vacuum, exactly 299,792,458 m/s, is one of the fundamental constants of nature.

== Electromagnetic spectrum ==
{{Main|Electromagnetic spectrum}}
Visible light occupies a narrow band of the [[electromagnetic spectrum]], with wavelengths roughly 380–750 nm (frequencies ~400–790 THz). This corresponds to colours from violet (shortest wavelength) through blue, green, yellow, orange, to red (longest wavelength).

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Colour !! Wavelength (nm) !! Frequency (THz)
|-
| Violet || 380–450 || 670–790
|-
| Blue || 450–485 || 620–670
|-
| Cyan || 485–500 || 600–620
|-
| Green || 500–565 || 530–600
|-
| Yellow || 565–590 || 510–530
|-
| Orange || 590–625 || 480–510
|-
| Red || 625–750 || 400–480
|}

== Speed of light ==
{{Main|Speed of light}}
In vacuum, light travels at exactly 299,792,458 m/s, denoted <math>c</math>. In transparent matter, light travels more slowly, at speed <math>v = c/n</math>, where <math>n</math> is the [[refractive index]] of the medium. The ratio of <math>c</math> to <math>v</math> determines the angle of [[refraction]] by [[Snell's law]].

== Optics ==
{{Main|Optics}}
The study of light is called [[optics]]. Key phenomena include:
* '''[[Reflection (physics)|Reflection]]''' — light bounces off a surface
* '''[[Refraction]]''' — light bends when entering a different medium
* '''[[Diffraction]]''' — light bends around obstacles and spreads through apertures
* '''[[Interference (wave propagation)|Interference]]''' — superposition of light waves producing bright and dark fringes
* '''[[Polarization (waves)|Polarization]]''' — orientation of the electric field oscillations
* '''[[Dispersion (optics)|Dispersion]]''' — different wavelengths refract at different angles (e.g., rainbow formation)

== Light sources ==
* [[Thermal radiation]] — [[black-body radiation]], e.g., [[incandescent light bulb]]s, the [[Sun]]
* [[Luminescence]] — [[fluorescence]], [[phosphorescence]], [[chemiluminescence]]
* [[Laser]] — coherent, monochromatic, directional light
* [[Gas discharge]] — [[neon sign]]s, [[fluorescent lamp]]s
* [[Light-emitting diode]] (LED) — semiconductor light sources

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Optics]]
* [[Photon]]
* [[Photoelectric effect]]
* [[Speed of light]]
* [[Wave–particle duality]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Light| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetic radiation]]
[[Category:Optics]]
